scope: Close vuln-source-parity-v1 M5 Bucket A Rust subset (4 carry-forwards: rust_command_injection_positive, rust_deserialization_positive, rust_path_traversal_positive, rust_ssrf_positive). Reframe C from vuln-migration-v1 plan §0. Replace the early-return dispatch at crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs:368-370 (which currently routes .rs files exclusively to analyze_rust_file's UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic line scanner) with a dual-dispatch shape: canonical scan_vulnerabilities runs on ALL extensions including .rs; analyze_rust_file additionally runs on .rs and emits the 3 Rust-specific smell variants (UnsafeCode, MemorySafety, Panic) plus 2 overlapping categories (SqlInjection, CommandInjection); a domain-aware dedupe_overlap helper drops line-scanner SqlInjection/CommandInjection findings on (line, vuln_type) tuples already covered by canonical (preserves smell-only findings unconditionally). Concurrently, ATOMIC with the dispatch flip, extend RUST_AST_SINKS HttpRequest member_patterns at taint.rs:2464-2477 to include reqwest::blocking::get / reqwest::blocking::Client — required to close rust_ssrf_positive (the ONLY of the 4 fixtures with a sink-bank gap surfaced by vuln-source-parity-v1 M1 investigation). NO new VulnType / TaintSinkType / TaintSourceType enum variants. NO public-API shape change. Recommended Option C from plan.md §1; Options A (no dedup) / B (canonical-first fallback) / D (port line scanner to AST) explicitly rejected per §3.
closes_carry_forward: vuln-source-parity-v1 M5 Bucket A Rust subset (4 tests: rust_command_injection_positive, rust_deserialization_positive, rust_path_traversal_positive, rust_ssrf_positive)
enables_followons: v0.2.x publish unblock (vuln_migration_v1_red 162/166 GREEN post-M3, leaving only Bucket B nested-constructor 3 + Bucket A Ruby backtick 1 = 4 retained RED)

validator_mandates.red_first_harness_required: The 4 RED tests in crates/tldr-cli/tests/vuln_migration_v1_red.rs (rust_*_positive at lines 1003, 1417, 1647, 1923) are the binary-verifiable success contract. NO new tests authored by this milestone. M1 captures RED state at HEAD; M2 stop-threshold gates on all 4 transitioning GREEN; M3 binary-smoke verifies independently. The 4 string-literal-FP regression-guards (rust_*_string_literal_fp at 1014, 1428, 1658, 1934) MUST stay GREEN at every milestone gate.
validator_mandates.no_public_api_change: VulnFinding struct shape (crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/types.rs) unchanged. map_core_vuln_type exhaustive-match contract preserved (no _ arm — guards against silent variant relabel per closing-#11). tldr_core::security::vuln::scan_vulnerabilities signature unchanged. Public-API surface binary-compatible pre/post M2.
validator_mandates.no_enum_variant_extension: VulnType (CLI + core), TaintSinkType, TaintSourceType — no new variants. If any variant gap surfaces during M2 implementation that would require a new variant, route to a separate enum-extension milestone.
validator_mandates.atomic_dispatch_commit: M2 ships dispatch flip (analyze_file body change) + dedupe_overlap helper (new private fn in same file) + SSRF bank patch (RUST_AST_SINKS HttpRequest member_patterns extension) + doc-comment update at vuln.rs:354-365 (retire Reframe C carry-forward note) in a SINGLE commit. Splitting creates intermediate states with regressions per plan.md §8: (a) dispatch flip without bank patch leaves rust_ssrf_positive RED; (b) bank patch without dispatch flip is dead code unreachable for .rs; (c) dispatch flip without dedupe produces 2x CommandInjection findings on overlapping lines.
validator_mandates.string_literal_regression_zero_new_fps: All 4 rust_*_string_literal_fp tests REMAIN GREEN. Verified empirically at planning time (each FP fixture lacks env_var sources, taint sinks, and line-scanner triggers; both layers emit zero on FP fixtures). M2 stop-threshold gates explicitly: `cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --test vuln_migration_v1_red rust_` must show 4/4 string-literal-FP tests GREEN.
validator_mandates.e2e_test_preservation_mandatory: All 36 test_e2e_* tests at crates/tldr-core/src/security/vuln.rs:1568-2100 must remain GREEN. Pre-existing regression-guard from vuln-migration-v1.
validator_mandates.test_analyze_rust_unit_tests_preserved: All 9 #[test] fns in the `commands::remaining::vuln::tests` module at crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs:925-1037 must remain GREEN post-merge (A4 amendment per premortem dab0766). The 9 fns are: test_vuln_type_cwe_mapping, test_vuln_type_severity, test_collect_files_includes_rust, test_analyze_rust_detects_unsafe_without_safety_comment, test_analyze_rust_detects_command_and_sql_patterns, test_analyze_rust_detects_transmute_usage, test_analyze_rust_detects_raw_pointer_operation, test_analyze_rust_detects_unwrap_in_non_test_code, test_analyze_rust_detects_unchecked_bytes_patterns. Of these, 6 prefix-match `test_analyze_rust_detects_*` (line-scanner emit-category coverage); 3 are auxiliary (test_vuln_type_cwe_mapping, test_vuln_type_severity, test_collect_files_includes_rust). Regression guard is the FULL 9, run via `cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --lib commands::remaining::vuln::tests`. They call analyze_rust_file directly; M2's dispatch-only change does not touch analyze_rust_file's body. M2 + M3 stop-thresholds verify all 9 GREEN.
validator_mandates.bank_addition_additive_only: The SSRF bank patch (RUST_AST_SINKS HttpRequest member_patterns extension at taint.rs:2464-2477) is PURELY ADDITIVE — adds 2 new member_patterns ('', 'reqwest::blocking::get') and ('', 'reqwest::blocking::Client'); does NOT remove existing patterns. M2 verification report MUST diff the HttpRequest member_patterns array before/after and enumerate added entries explicitly.
validator_mandates.dedupe_logic_premortem_completed: /premortem run at commit dab0766; verdict GO_WITH_AMENDMENTS. 5 non-blocking amendments A1-A5 applied to plan.md and this dispatch-contract.json. Dedupe logic verified architecturally sound; predicate `c.line == line_f.line && c.vuln_type == line_f.vuln_type` correctly handles single-line overlaps and different-VulnType same-line cases; multi-line chain edge case (T5) acknowledged and routed to M3-dedupe-verification.json FIXTURE-2 for empirical line-key precision check (not blocking; documented as known limitation if line keys diverge). Premortem also surfaced 2 elephants requiring documentation only (not scope expansion): R8 wildcard-get bank pattern (T2/E1; M3 quantifies FP rate via 5 synthetic fixtures per A2) and R2 sub-elephant is_rust_test_file Panic suppression mismatch between fixtures vs production code (E3; carry-forward acknowledged for post-publish hardening). M2 stress-test cases for kraken implementation: line-scanner emit on line 5 + canonical emit on line 5 same VulnType (must collapse to 1); line-scanner emit on line 5 + canonical emit on line 6 same VulnType (must keep 2 — different lines); line-scanner UnsafeCode on line 5 + canonical CommandInjection on line 5 (must keep 2 — different VulnTypes).
validator_mandates.no_perf_regression: Post-M2 .rs files cost roughly the same as .py/.js/etc. (which already use canonical pipeline). M3 binary smoke captures wall-clock on representative Rust fixtures; if median time-per-file exceeds current Python median by >2x, surface in M3-report.json and triage. The structural perf carry-forward from vuln-migration-v1 M3-CF-02 (avg 17.18× M1) is documented but not addressed here.
validator_mandates.carry_forward_max_0: All 4 Bucket A Rust subset tests must close in M2. Aggregate carry-forward count for THIS milestone is 0 — no exceptions. (Bucket B — 3 nested-constructor tests cpp/java/scala_deserialization_positive — is OUT OF SCOPE here; closes in var-extract-nested-constructor-v1. Bucket A Ruby backtick — 1 test ruby_command_injection_positive — closes in ruby-backtick-extraction-v1.)
validator_mandates.staging_method_explicit: Each commit stages exactly the listed files via `git add <path>...`. NO `git add -A` / `git add .`. Cargo.lock NEVER staged (`git checkout HEAD -- Cargo.lock` before each commit if dirty). Pre-existing dirty modifications to UNRELATED continuum/autonomous/* artifacts (per `git status` at planning time) are NOT staged by this milestone.
validator_mandates.no_push_no_publish_no_version_bump: All milestones internal. Local tag `rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1` only at M5. NO `cargo publish`, NO `git push`, NO version bump in Cargo.toml. USER STANDING RULE.

staging_method.verification: git show HEAD --stat | awk '/continuum\/autonomous\// { print }' | grep -v 'rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1-plan/' && echo LEAK || echo STAGING-OK
staging_method.verification_note: Mirrors vuln-source-parity-v1 staging convention. Per premortem RM-4 from sibling plan: PCRE negative lookahead is unsupported by macOS BSD grep -E; use awk + grep -v pipeline. Manual `git show HEAD --stat` audit is canonical.
staging_method.rationale: Prevents accidental staging of unrelated continuum/autonomous artifacts (especially the existing unstaged modifications to vuln-migration-v1-plan/, regex-removal-v1-plan/, etc. visible at planning-time `git status`). Cargo.lock NEVER staged.

milestones.title: Investigation, RED capture, line-scanner emit-category audit

Build release binary with --features semantic; for each of 4 positive fixtures run BOTH `tldr taint <fixture> handler` AND `tldr vuln <fixture> --lang rust`; record output verbatim in M1-line-scanner-audit.json. Confirm M1-investigation.json claims still hold (taint pipeline detects source/sink/flow on cmd/path/deser; ssrf has bank gap on reqwest::blocking::get). Run `tldr vuln` on the 4 string-literal-FP fixtures; record finding counts (expecting zero) — M2 regression baseline. Capture the 9 test_analyze_rust_* unit tests passing.

stop_thresholds: All 4 RED tests confirmed RED at HEAD: rust_command_injection_positive, rust_deserialization_positive, rust_path_traversal_positive, rust_ssrf_positive.
All 4 string-literal-FP regression-guards confirmed GREEN at HEAD.

9/9 #[test] fns in `commands::remaining::vuln::tests` module confirmed GREEN at HEAD (the full 9: test_vuln_type_cwe_mapping, test_vuln_type_severity, test_collect_files_includes_rust, test_analyze_rust_detects_unsafe_without_safety_comment, test_analyze_rust_detects_command_and_sql_patterns, test_analyze_rust_detects_transmute_usage, test_analyze_rust_detects_raw_pointer_operation, test_analyze_rust_detects_unwrap_in_non_test_code, test_analyze_rust_detects_unchecked_bytes_patterns); A4 amendment per premortem dab0766.
tldr taint output on each of the 4 positive fixtures recorded verbatim in M1-line-scanner-audit.json.

tldr vuln output on each of the 4 FP fixtures recorded (zero findings — pre-M2 regression baseline).

A5 BASELINE-DRIFT CHECK (per premortem dab0766 E2): run `git log --oneline 5d46628..HEAD -- crates/` as the FIRST action of M1 to detect any source-code drift between the planning baseline (5d46628) and M1 launch HEAD. If output is empty, record `baseline_drift: none` in M1-report.json. If non-empty, M1 worker MUST (a) enumerate each commit's source-file diff, (b) re-run the 158/166 GREEN baseline `cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --test vuln_migration_v1_red` count, (c) record actual baseline GREEN count in M1-report.json, (d) flag any divergence from plan's 158/166 expectation, (e) adjust M2 expected post-merge GREEN count accordingly (planned: 162/166 = baseline + 4).

milestones.title: ATOMIC dispatch flip + dedupe helper + SSRF bank patch
milestones.scope: In crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs (function analyze_file at line 366-419): replace the early-return at line 368-370 with the dual-dispatch shape from plan.md §5. Add a new private helper `dedupe_overlap(line_findings: &mut Vec<VulnFinding>, canonical: &[VulnFinding])` immediately after `analyze_rust_file` — drops line-scanner SqlInjection/CommandInjection findings whose (line, vuln_type) tuple matches a canonical finding; preserves UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic unconditionally. Update the doc-comment at lines 354-365 to retire the Reframe C carry-forward note (replace with a citation of this milestone). Concurrently, in crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs at the RUST_AST_SINKS HttpRequest entry (line 2464-2477): extend member_patterns to include ('', 'reqwest::blocking::get') and ('', 'reqwest::blocking::Client'). ATOMIC: dispatch + dedupe + bank + doc all in ONE commit per validator_mandate atomic_dispatch_commit.
milestones.loc_delta_estimate: +30 to +50 LOC source code (~10 LOC dispatch shape, ~15 LOC dedupe_overlap helper, +2 LOC bank entry, ~10 LOC doc-comment update)
stop_thresholds: rust_command_injection_positive transitions GREEN.
rust_deserialization_positive transitions GREEN.
rust_path_traversal_positive transitions GREEN.
rust_ssrf_positive transitions GREEN.
0 currently-GREEN tests transition RED — verified by `cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --test vuln_migration_v1_red` (expect 162/166 GREEN, was 158/166).
All 4 rust_*_string_literal_fp tests STAY GREEN.

All 9 #[test] fns in `commands::remaining::vuln::tests` module at remaining/vuln.rs:925-1037 STAY GREEN (run via `cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --lib commands::remaining::vuln::tests`; A4 amendment per premortem dab0766).
All 36 test_e2e_* tests in tldr-core/security/vuln.rs STAY GREEN.
cargo check --workspace PASS.
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings PASS.
cargo test --workspace PASS (no regression in tldr-core or other crates).
ATOMIC: single commit. Dispatch flip + dedupe helper + SSRF bank entry + doc-comment update ship together with NO intermediate commit.
Diff verification: `git diff HEAD~1 crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs | grep '^+.*reqwest::blocking'` shows exactly 2 new member_pattern entries (no removals).
Diff verification: `git diff HEAD~1 crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs | grep -E '^\+.*fn (dedupe_overlap|analyze_file)'` confirms both function signatures present.

milestones.title: Verification, binary smoke, dedupe behavior verification
milestones.scope: Build release binary with --features semantic at the M2 SHA. Smoke-test against all 4 RED-now-GREEN positive fixtures (each must produce ≥1 finding of the asserted vuln_type) AND all 4 string-literal-FP fixtures (each must produce zero findings — preserves the closing-#24 zero-FP guarantee). Run a representative non-Rust file (python/sql_injection_positive.py) through the binary to verify non-Rust dispatch is unchanged. Construct an ad-hoc dedupe-verification fixture (NOT added to the test corpus) with both canonical CommandInjection trigger and line-scanner CommandInjection trigger on the same line; confirm exactly ONE CommandInjection finding emerges (dedupe verified empirically). Capture JSON/SARIF output schema diffs for .rs file findings pre/post M2 — confirm shape unchanged, count delta only.
milestones.loc_delta_estimate: 0 source code; ~200 LOC across 3 report JSONs
stop_thresholds: cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --test vuln_migration_v1_red: 162/166 GREEN (was 158/166 at HEAD; +4 from M2). Adjust target if M1 A5 baseline-drift check surfaced divergence from 158/166.
vuln_migration_v1_composite_red 1/1 passing.
All 36 test_e2e_* tests in tldr-core remain GREEN.
All 9 #[test] fns in `commands::remaining::vuln::tests` module remain GREEN post-merge (A4).
Binary smoke: all 4 RED→GREEN transitions verified independently of cargo test.
Binary smoke: all 4 FP fixtures still produce zero findings.
Binary smoke: python_sql_injection_positive.py still produces ≥1 sql_injection finding (non-Rust dispatch sanity).

A2/A3 DEDUPE-VERIFICATION (per premortem dab0766 T1/T5): the 4 RED fixtures CANNOT be used to verify the dedupe predicate (line scanner emits 0 findings on all 4 — see plan §3 A1 correction). M3-dedupe-verification.json MUST capture results for THREE constructed synthetic fixtures (NOT added to the test corpus): (a) FIXTURE-1 single-line same-vuln-type overlap: `let cmd = std::env::var("K").unwrap(); std::process::Command::new("sh").arg(&cmd).status().unwrap();` with `Command::new` and `.arg(&cmd)` on ONE line WITHOUT preceding `.arg("...")` — line scanner CommandInjection emits AND canonical CommandInjection emits on same line → dedupe MUST collapse to 1 finding. (b) FIXTURE-2 multi-line same-vuln-type chain: `Command::new("sh")` on line N then `.arg(&cmd)` on line N+2 (intervening blank/comment line). Verify line-key precision: line scanner emits at `command_block_start_line.max(line_number)` = N+2; canonical may emit at the call_expression line (N) OR field_expression line (N+2). If both layers emit at the same line → dedupe collapses to 1; if line keys diverge → dedupe FAILS to collapse → 2 findings emerge (acknowledged limitation; document in M3-dedupe-verification.json with non-additive-resolution rationale; do NOT widen dedupe predicate within this milestone). (c) FIXTURE-3 different-vuln-type same-line: line-scanner UnsafeCode trigger AND canonical CommandInjection on the same line K → dedupe MUST keep 2 findings (different VulnTypes; predicate filters by vuln_type equality).

A2 WILDCARD-GET FP QUANTIFICATION (per premortem dab0766 T2/E1, R8 in plan §9): construct 5 synthetic Rust fixtures exercising newly-live RUST_AST_SINKS patterns post-M2: (i) HashMap::get on tainted key, (ii) Vec::get on tainted index, (iii) Option::get with tainted, (iv) handler with reqwest::blocking::get tainted (the SSRF closure path; expected 1 Ssrf finding — TRUE POSITIVE), (v) std::fs::read_to_string on tainted (FileOpen path). Run `tldr vuln <fixture> --lang rust --format json` on each. Record FP count per pattern in M3-binary-smoke.json. If wildcard `("*", "get")` produces FP rate >10% on synthetic patterns (i)/(ii)/(iii), document as known carry-forward and surface as bank-tightening follow-on milestone — do NOT adjust the wildcard within this milestone (atomic-commit boundary preserved).
Dedupe verification FIXTURE-1 produces exactly 1 CommandInjection finding (not 2).
Dedupe verification FIXTURE-3 produces exactly 2 findings (line-scanner UnsafeCode + canonical CommandInjection — different VulnTypes).
JSON/SARIF schema diff: zero structural changes; finding count delta only.
M3-report.json records SHA at which verification was run, all metric counts, baseline-drift check result from M1 (A5), and verbatim binary smoke transcripts.
M3-dedupe-verification.json captures FIXTURE-1, FIXTURE-2, FIXTURE-3 source code, command-line invocations, and verbatim JSON output per A2/A3.
M3-binary-smoke.json captures the 5 wildcard-get FP-quantification fixtures with FP counts per RUST_AST_SINKS pattern per A2 amendment.

milestones.title: Local tag rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1
milestones.scope: Apply local tag `rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1` at the M4 commit SHA. NO push, NO publish, NO version bump.
stop_thresholds: Local tag `rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1` applied at the M4 commit SHA.
`git tag --list 'rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1'` returns the tag.
No push, no publish, no version bump.

rollback_plan.if_M2_fails: git revert <M2_sha>. The atomic commit means revert is single-step. Dispatch flip + dedupe helper + SSRF bank entry + doc-comment update revert atomically. The 4 RED tests return to RED state; no orphan partial states. No downstream impact on test_e2e_* or string-literal regression-guards.
rollback_plan.if_M2_breaks_string_literal_fp: git revert <M2_sha>. Same atomic revert. The 4 string-literal-FP tests return to GREEN; the 4 positive tests return to RED. Triage the dedupe predicate — most likely root cause for FP fixture finding emission.
rollback_plan.if_M2_breaks_test_analyze_rust_unit_test: git revert <M2_sha>. The 9 unit tests are decoupled from analyze_file (they call analyze_rust_file directly). If they regress, M2 must have inadvertently modified analyze_rust_file's body — surface the root cause and re-implement scoping the change to analyze_file only.
rollback_plan.if_M3_surfaces_unanticipated_carry_forwa: Document in M3-report.json with non-additive-resolution rationale; do NOT extend milestone scope. The 4 carry-forward closure is the binary contract for this milestone.

success_summary_post_m5.carry_forward_aggregate_post_m5: 4 retained RED (Bucket B 3 nested-constructor + Bucket A Ruby 1) — DOWN from 8 retained RED at vuln-source-parity-v1 M5 (Bucket A Rust 4 closed by this milestone).
success_summary_post_m5.follow_on_milestones_remaining: var-extract-nested-constructor-v1 (closes Bucket B 3); ruby-backtick-extraction-v1 (closes Bucket A Ruby 1).

post_publish_unblock: After M5 lands, the v0.2.x release group has 162/166 vuln_migration_v1_red GREEN. Combined with var-extract-nested-constructor-v1 (closes 3 Bucket B) + ruby-backtick-extraction-v1 (closes 1 Ruby), all 166/166 GREEN is achievable. The published binary's `tldr vuln` will detect Rust taint flows for the 4 (vuln_type, fixture) pairs the post-vuln-source-parity-v1 binary silently missed.
